# Trilogy CLI - AI Agent Usage Guide

## Overview

Trilogy is a semantic ETL and reporting tool providing a SQL-like language with
optimizations. This CLI enables workspace management, script execution, testing,
and data ingestion.

Defaults are managed by a trilogy.toml config file; convention
will be to manage connection info there. CLI flags available for exceptions.

## Quick Start

```bash
# Initialize a new workspace
trilogy init [path]

# Run a script
trilogy run script.preql dialect [connecti
...[truncated 35814 bytes]...
de', 'longitude' and 'currency' are all of type 'float', but have additional meaning.

A typical trilogy query only needs to import one file (the fact) and will use dot-references to pull in dimensions.

In the rare case of merging two fact domains, use a MERGE statement to merge share dimensions concepts another; ex `merge concept_a into ~concept_b`. This marks concept_a as being identical to a partial subset
of concept_b, enabling discovery to bridge the two. If a dimenion is accessible through a fact, via abc.def - use that instead of merging".

# Trilogy CLI - AI Agent Usage Guide

## Overview

Trilogy is a semantic ETL and reporting tool providing a SQL-like language with
optimizations. This CLI enables workspace management, script execution, testing,
and data ingestion.

Defaults are managed by a trilogy.toml config file; convention
will be to manage connection info there. CLI flags available for exceptions.

## Quick Start

```bash
# Initialize a new workspace
trilogy init [path]

# Run a script
trilogy run script.preql dialect [connecti
...[truncated 35814 bytes]...
de', 'longitude' and 'currency' are all of type 'float', but have additional meaning.

A typical trilogy query only needs to import one file (the fact) and will use dot-references to pull in dimensions.

In the rare case of merging two fact domains, use a MERGE statement to merge share dimensions concepts another; ex `merge concept_a into ~concept_b`. This marks concept_a as being identical to a partial subset
of concept_b, enabling discovery to bridge the two. If a dimenion is accessible through a fact, via abc.def - use that instead of merging".

# Trilogy CLI - AI Agent Usage Guide

## Overview

Trilogy is a semantic ETL and reporting tool providing a SQL-like language with
optimizations. This CLI enables workspace management, script execution, testing,
and data ingestion.

Defaults are managed by a trilogy.toml config file; convention
will be to manage connection info there. CLI flags available for exceptions.

## Quick Start

```bash
# Initialize a new workspace
trilogy init [path]

# Run a script
trilogy run script.preql dialect [connecti
...[truncated 35814 bytes]...
de', 'longitude' and 'currency' are all of type 'float', but have additional meaning.

A typical trilogy query only needs to import one file (the fact) and will use dot-references to pull in dimensions.

In the rare case of merging two fact domains, use a MERGE statement to merge share dimensions concepts another; ex `merge concept_a into ~concept_b`. This marks concept_a as being identical to a partial subset
of concept_b, enabling discovery to bridge the two. If a dimenion is accessible through a fact, via abc.def - use that instead of merging".

USER/TOOL_RESULT: {"tool": "read_file", "result": "# Store channel returns (TPC-DS store_returns): item-level returns joined
# back to the originating store_sales row by ticket_number + item.id. Exposes
# return_amount; pair with store_sales.net_paid for return-rate analysis
# (q17, q25, q29).

import store as store;
import store_sales as store_sales;
import item as item;
import date as return_date;
import time as time;
import customer as customer;
import customer_demographic as customer_demographic;

property <item.id, store_sales.ticket_number>.return_amount float;

datasource store_returns (
    SR_RETURNED_DATE_SK: return_date.id,
    SR_RETURN_TIME_SK: time.id,
    SR_ITEM_SK: item.id,
    SR_CUSTOMER_SK: customer.id,
    SR_CDEMO_SK: ?customer_demographic.id,
    SR_RETURN_AMT: return_amount,
    SR_TICKET_NUMBER: store_sales.ticket_number,
    SR_STORE_SK: store.id,
)
grain (item.id, store_sales.ticket_number)
address store_returns;
"}

# Trilogy CLI - AI Agent Usage Guide

## Overview

Trilogy is a semantic ETL and reporting tool providing a SQL-like language with
optimizations. This CLI enables workspace management, script execution, testing,
and data ingestion.

Defaults are managed by a trilogy.toml config file; convention
will be to manage connection info there. CLI flags available for exceptions.

## Quick Start

```bash
# Initialize a new workspace
trilogy init [path]

# Run a script
trilogy run script.preql dialect [connecti

de', 'longitude' and 'currency' are all of type 'float', but have additional meaning.

A typical trilogy query only needs to import one file (the fact) and will use dot-references to pull in dimensions.

In the rare case of merging two fact domains, use a MERGE statement to merge share dimensions concepts another; ex `merge concept_a into ~concept_b`. This marks concept_a as being identical to a partial subset
of concept_b, enabling discovery to bridge the two. If a dimenion is accessible through a fact, via abc.def - use that instead of merging".

import item as item;
import date as date;
import date as return_date;
import time as time;
import time as return_time;
import customer as customer;

...[truncated 1680 bytes]...
d,
)
grain (item.id, ticket_number)
address store_sales;

datasource store_returns (
    SR_RETURNED_DATE_SK: return_date.id,
    SR_RETURN_TIME_SK: return_time.id,
    SR_ITEM_SK: ~item.id,
    SR_CUSTOMER_SK: return_customer.id,
    SR_RETURN_AMT: return_amount,
    SR_RETURN_QUANTITY: return_quantity,
    SR_TICKET_NUMBER: ~ticket_number,
    SR_STORE_SK: return_store.id,

    raw('''SR_RETURN_TIME_SK IS NOT NULL'''): is_returned,
)
grain (item.id, ticket_number)
address store_returns;
"}

key id int;

properties id (

    marital_status string,
    education_status string,
    purchase_estimate string,
    credit_rating string,
    dependent_count int,
    employed_dependent_count int,
    college_dependent_count int,
);

datasource customer_demographics (
    CD_DEMO_SK: id,
    CD_GENDER: gender,
    CD_MARITAL_STATUS: marital_status,
    CD_EDUCATION_STATUS: education_status,
    CD_PURCHASE_ESTIMATE: purchase_estimate,
    CD_CREDIT_RATING: credit_rating,
    CD_DEP_COUNT: dependent_count,
    CD_DEP_EMPLOYED_COUNT: employed_dependent_count,

)
grain (id)
address customer_demographics;
"}

key id int;

properties id (

);

datasource promotion (

    P_CHANNEL_TV: channel_tv,

    P_CHANNEL_PRESS: channel_press,
)
grain (id)
address promotion;
"}

# Trilogy CLI - AI Agent Usage Guide

## Overview

Trilogy is a semantic ETL and reporting tool providing a SQL-like language with
optimizations. This CLI enables workspace management, script execution, testing,
and data ingestion.

Defaults are managed by a trilogy.toml config file; convention
will be to manage connection info there. CLI flags available for exceptions.

## Quick Start

```bash
# Initialize a new workspace
trilogy init [path]

# Run a script
trilogy run script.preql dialect [connecti
...[truncated 35814 bytes]...
de', 'longitude' and 'currency' are all of type 'float', but have additional meaning.

A typical trilogy query only needs to import one file (the fact) and will use dot-references to pull in dimensions.

In the rare case of merging two fact domains, use a MERGE statement to merge share dimensions concepts another; ex `merge concept_a into ~concept_b`. This marks concept_a as being identical to a partial subset
of concept_b, enabling discovery to bridge the two. If a dimenion is accessible through a fact, via abc.def - use that instead of merging".

USER/TOOL_RESULT: {"tool": "read_file", "result": "# Customer demographic dimension (TPC-DS customer_demographics): gender,
# marital and education status, credit rating, purchase-estimate bucket,
# and dependent counts (total / employed / college). Referenced from
# customer.demographics and from the *_CDEMO_SK columns on sales facts.

key id int;

properties id (
    gender string,
    marital_status string,
    education_status string,
    purchase_estimate string,
    credit_rating string,
    dependent_count int,
    employed_dependent_count int,
    college_dependent_count int,
);

datasource customer_demographics (
    CD_DEMO_SK: id,
    CD_GENDER: gender,
    CD_MARITAL_STATUS: marital_status,
    CD_EDUCATION_STATUS: education_status,
    CD_PURCHASE_ESTIMATE: purchase_estimate,
    CD_CREDIT_RATING: credit_rating,
    CD_DEP_COUNT: dependent_count,
    CD_DEP_EMPLOYED_COUNT: employed_dependent_count,
    CD_DEP_COLLEGE_COUNT: college_dependent_count,
)
grain (id)
address customer_demographics;
"}

)
grain (id)
address customer_address;
"}

# Trilogy CLI - AI Agent Usage Guide

## Overview

Trilogy is a semantic ETL and reporting tool providing a SQL-like language with
optimizations. This CLI enables workspace management, script execution, testing,
and data ingestion.

Defaults are managed by a trilogy.toml config file; convention
will be to manage connection info there. CLI flags available for exceptions.

## Quick Start

```bash
# Initialize a new workspace
trilogy init [path]

# Run a script
trilogy run script.preql dialect [connecti
...[truncated 35814 bytes]...
de', 'longitude' and 'currency' are all of type 'float', but have additional meaning.

A typical trilogy query only needs to import one file (the fact) and will use dot-references to pull in dimensions.

In the rare case of merging two fact domains, use a MERGE statement to merge share dimensions concepts another; ex `merge concept_a into ~concept_b`. This marks concept_a as being identical to a partial subset
of concept_b, enabling discovery to bridge the two. If a dimenion is accessible through a fact, via abc.def - use that instead of merging".

# Trilogy CLI - AI Agent Usage Guide

## Overview

Trilogy is a semantic ETL and reporting tool with a streamlined
SQL-like syntax. This CLI enables workspace management, script execution, testing,
and data ingestion.

## Quick Start

```bash
# Initialize a new workspace
trilogy init [path]

# Run a script
trilogy run script.preql dialect [connection_args...]

# Run unit tests (mocked datasources)
trilogy unit script.preql

# Run integration tests (real connections)
trilogy integration script.preq
...[truncated 31361 bytes]...
ank rollup subtotals/leaves with a SINGLE `rank(a,b) over (partition by level, parent ...)` — not separate ranks per level
- `staged-membership` — compute a membership set in a `rowset` (keys meeting a count/HAVING), then filter the main query with `<key> in <rowset>.<col>`
- `correlated-exists-via-grouped-counts` — translate `EXISTS other` / `NOT EXISTS other matching` over the same model into two `count(...) by <grain>` compared in `where` (`> 1` = another exists, `= 1` = no other matches) — don't filter on a boolean-of-aggregate



--- stderr ---
"}
